title: Speed curves upward during the burn — steeper as the rocket lightens
setup:
# --- plot helper (hidden) — rocket speed over a burn ---
# Each curve steps the burn using the student's OWN mass_after to find the
# rocket's mass at each instant (lighter -> steeper). Falls back per curve to
# the built-in mass model until mass_after works.
def __burn_ref(m_full, m_empty, ve, rate, dt, every):
    m = m_full; v = 0.0; t = 0.0
    pts = [[0.0, 0.0]]
    i = 0
    while m > m_empty:
        a = ve * rate / m
        v += a * dt; m -= rate * dt; t += dt
        if i % every == 0:
            pts.append([round(t, 2), round(v, 2)])
        i += 1
    pts.append([round(t, 2), round(v, 2)])
    return pts

def __burn_student(m_full, m_empty, ve, rate, dt, every):
    v = 0.0; t = 0.0; i = 0
    pts = [[0.0, 0.0]]
    max_iter = int((m_full - m_empty) / (rate * dt)) + 5
    for _ in range(max_iter):
        m = mass_after(m_full, rate, t)   # <- the STUDENT's function
        if m <= m_empty:
            break
        a = ve * rate / m
        v += a * dt; t += dt
        if i % every == 0:
            pts.append([round(t, 2), round(v, 2)])
        i += 1
    pts.append([round(t, 2), round(v, 2)])
    return pts

def __plot(_):
    series = []
    for mf, me, ve, rate, hot, label in (
        (100, 40, 3000, 2, False, "half fuel (100 -> 40 kg)"),
        (50, 10, 2500, 1, True, "mostly fuel (50 -> 10 kg)"),
    ):
        try:
            p = __burn_student(mf, me, ve, rate, 0.05, 10)
            if p and len(p) > 2 and p[-1][1] > 0:
                series.append({"name": label, "points": p, "highlight": hot})
                continue
        except Exception:
            pass
        series.append({"name": label, "points": __burn_ref(mf, me, ve, rate, 0.05, 10), "highlight": hot})
    return series
